assessability_rule: A capitulum must be recognisable as one discrete head in the source photograph
intended_use: Detection gate and photo-level visible-head count
notes: This is not anthesis and not a whole-plant flower count.

assessability_rule: Score only when corollas or florets can be evaluated from the crop
intended_use: Separate flowering-stage QC before floral trait annotation
do_not_infer_from: Phenology text or a detector box
notes: Do not call buds or senescent heads anthesis without visible evidence.

required_visual_context: head plus peduncle and proximal stem
assessability_rule: The head-peduncle axis must be visible; otherwise use unassessable
intended_use: Species-level description compared with individual-level image observations
do_not_infer_from: Species identity alone or a head-only crop
notes: Do not collapse nodding and pendant unless a preregistered binary analysis explicitly defines the collapse.

assessability_rule: Use unassessable for severe colour cast backlighting or insufficient visible corolla
intended_use: Documented colour states and image-level colour observations
notes: Image class is descriptive; spectral and pigment data are required for biochemical claims.

required_visual_context: side or oblique view showing the involucre outline
assessability_rule: Do not score from a frontal view that hides the outline
intended_use: Comparative morphology and image-level shape structure
notes: This is an observational category not a replacement for geometric morphometrics.

assessability_rule: Score only visible surfaces; multiple states may be recorded
intended_use: Description extraction and high-resolution image audit
notes: Use one row per visible state when allow_multiple is true.

required_visual_context: bract tips visible at sufficient resolution
assessability_rule: Do not treat hidden or blurred tips as unarmed
intended_use: Comparative morphology and defensive trait hypotheses
do_not_infer_from: Taxon name or a distant crop
notes: State thresholds must be documented separately if measured quantitatively.

required_visual_context: bract orientation visible in side or oblique view
assessability_rule: Use unassessable when perspective cannot distinguish states
intended_use: Comparative morphology and image-level bract architecture
notes: Use mixed only for real within-head heterogeneity rather than uncertainty.

required_visual_context: macro view of the relevant involucre or bract surface
assessability_rule: Absence is scoreable only when the relevant surface is clear and unobscured
intended_use: Generate targeted field and macro-image hypotheses
do_not_infer_from: Ordinary observer photograph or species name
notes: General iNaturalist photographs are usually unassessable for this trait.
